title: Tokyo Overpass Static
prompt: Create an original Japanese alternative rock track at 120 BPM in E minor, with sharp alternative-rock drive, melodic tension, and clean-to-fuzz guitar contrast. Use tight kick, crisp snare, bright hats, short tom fills; bass moves melodically under suspended chords with steady eighths. Guitars: clean arpeggios, crunchy chorus chords, octave leads, sudden fuzz bursts, plus subtle synth pad shadows and controlled feedback. Vocal texture: clear, emotional, slightly restrained, rising into the chorus. Structure: noise-and-riff intro, lean verse with bass and dry drums, pre-chorus with harmony guitar and snare lift, full chorus with wider chords, eight-bar bridge or solo break, then final chorus into tight outro. Production: polished but guitar-centered, dynamic, modern; mix/master: wide guitars, bright snare, vocal clarity, no over-polished pop sheen.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop
